source: SARS Income Tax Act No. 58 of 1962 — Section 6A (Medical Scheme Fees Tax Credit) — Budget Speech 25 February 2026
description: The Medical Scheme Fees Tax Credit (MSFTC) is a non-refundable tax credit that directly reduces PAYE withheld from an employee's salary. TY2027 restructures the credit: the first dependant rate is raised to equal the principal rate. Excess credit (where MSFTC > PAYE) is NOT paid out by the employer — the employee claims the balance on their annual ITR12 return.

formula.description: monthly_msftc = 376 + (376 if dependants >= 1 else 0) + (254 × max(0, dependants - 1))

paye_application.floor: PAYE cannot go below zero. If MSFTC > calculated_paye, net PAYE = R0.
paye_application.excess: Excess credit is NOT refundable by the employer. Employee claims it on ITR12.
paye_application.timing: Applied in the same pay period. Not carried forward by employer to next month.
paye_application.applied_by: PayrollOrchestrationService — after calling PayeCalculationEngine, before building PayrollResult

irp5_reporting.annual_value: Sum of monthly MSFTC credits applied across all pay periods in the tax year
irp5_reporting.note: Reported as a credit (positive value) in the tax withheld section. Reduces total employees tax shown on certificate.

payslip_display.label: Medical Tax Credit (Sec 6A)
payslip_display.placement: Shown as a credit in the PAYE deduction line — reduces PAYE withheld
payslip_display.example_format: PAYE before credit: R2,000 | Medical Tax Credit: (R752) | PAYE withheld: R1,248

employee_requirements.medical_scheme_membership: Employee must be a contributing member of a registered medical aid scheme (registered under the Medical Schemes Act)
employee_requirements.dependant_declaration: Employee must declare number of registered dependants to HR via written declaration (medical_scheme_dependants field in employee profile)
employee_requirements.change_notification: Employee must notify HR when dependant count changes (new dependant, dependant removed). Change applies from the following pay period.
employee_requirements.field_in_employee_profile: medical_scheme_dependants: integer (0 = principal only; 1 = principal + 1 dependant; etc.)

validation_rules: MSFTC applies only when employee is on a registered medical scheme (medical_scheme_name not null)
medical_scheme_dependants must be >= 0 (null treated as 0 if on scheme, 0 MSFTC if not on scheme)
net_paye >= 0 always — MSFTC cannot create a negative tax liability
Excess MSFTC is NOT carried forward or refunded by employer
On IRP5, code 4116 annual total must equal sum of monthly MSFTC credits applied

notes.ty2027_structural_change: TY2027 restructures the credit table: first dependant rate raised to R376 (equal to principal, up from R246). Each additional dependant drops slightly from R246 to R254 — but note R254 > R246, so all tiers increased. The structural change means the formula can no longer be simplified to 'principal + (246 × dependants)'. The MsfTcCalculationEngine must be updated to use the new formula.
notes.vs_ty2026: TY2026 amounts: principal R364, first_dep R246, each_additional R246. TY2026 doc: SARS_MSFTC_2026 (expired 28 Feb 2026).
notes.engine_architecture: MsfTcCalculationEngine takes: int numberOfDependants, SarsMsfTcRuleSet (from this document). Returns: MoneyZAR monthlyCredit.
notes.it3a_note: If employee has zero PAYE (IT3(a) certificate), MSFTC is still reported as code 4116 on the certificate. Employee uses it to reduce tax on ITR12.
